121224.explanation: We strive for equal representation but suitable candidates must be found.

121225.explanation: Decision-making in the municipality involves complex issues; a referendum with a simple question does not do them justice.

121230.explanation: We believe we must comply with the distribution law. We want to maintain control ourselves so we are not blindsided by the COA imposing a location on us.

121231.explanation: Our municipality has virtually no business activities requiring large amounts of seasonal labor. Housing on farm premises is not desired if this becomes an issue in the future.

121233.explanation: Burning should not be prohibited but the municipality must actively provide information about the burning guide and healthy burning, as regularly happens now in Groenlose Gids and Elna.

121238.explanation: Provided this fits within the environmental plan.

121240.explanation: The municipality already contributes financially. Additional funding will only be considered on an incidental basis under special circumstances.

121245.explanation: Sufficient supervision of, for example, drug use is important for local safety.

121246.explanation: However, only at times and places where problems are anticipated in advance.

121252.explanation: The CDA is convinced that working makes people happier than not participating. When people can do (volunteer) work, this is encouraged.

121254.explanation: We have increased some income thresholds, such as those for children (laptop purchase, etc.); we want to maintain this.

121257.explanation: This has been implemented on all roads except arterial roads.

121258.explanation: Unfortunately, there are major shortages in healthcare and this is not realistic.

121260.explanation: The property tax (ozb) may increase in line with inflation. We are still a municipality with one of the lowest property taxes in the Achterhoek region.

121261.explanation: The residents' charges in our municipality are relatively low. In addition, dog tax is not a target tax and is used for general financial coverage. Eliminating it would require other levies or taxes to rise or cuts to be made. We do not want either.

121262.explanation: There is no reason to do this now, but we are not fundamentally opposed to it.

121263.explanation: There is a need for more business parks for local companies. Therefore, we want to conduct research to determine the best location for a new business park. The Regional Planning Agreement shows that there is a need for more space for local and regional businesses.

121266.explanation: CDA agrees with this. We support the energy transition. Solar panels belong on the roofs of homes and business parks to make the most efficient use of the scarce available space. Existing parks and initiatives for this can remain, but we will not allow new initiatives for solar parks.

121269.explanation: If schools need support in this regard, the municipality can play a role.

121270.explanation: CDA is actually in favor of active preventive policies when it comes to drug use. Under the motto 'drugs dr’uut' (drugs out), we are working towards less drug use in Oost Gelre.

121271.explanation: Building must be based on need. The demand for social rental housing in Oost Gelre may differ from that in large cities, so we should not rigidly adhere to national standards.

121272.explanation: In times of severe housing shortages, such forms of rental should be allowed.

121273.explanation: In the larger centers, there must be sufficient parking for shoppers, but large parking areas should not disrupt the streetscape.

121274.explanation: This contributes to the appearance of the fortified town.

121275.explanation: The municipality and sports clubs have good rules regarding (the replacement of) the facilities.

121276.explanation: We have several large-scale events (Slag om Grolle, flower parade, etc.) that we are proud of.

121277.explanation: The municipality must actively implement preventive policies, especially among youth. But the government should not become too preachy.

121278.explanation: We must build in all centers-the small ones as well as in Lichtenvoorde and Groenlo.

121279.explanation: This fits within our policy: drugs dr’uut.

121280.explanation: We are in favor of the energy transition; wind turbines are part of that.

121224.explanation: GL-PvdA advocates for an inclusive society. It is desirable that the municipal executive reflects the diversity of society.

121225.explanation: It is desirable that citizens can actively contribute through participation in projects and plans. This has more value than a referendum.

121230.explanation: Oost Gelre has contributed in recent years to the reception of status holders, displaced persons (Ukrainians), and refugees (unaccompanied minor asylum seekers and family reunifications) and will also need to accommodate a group of asylum seekers.

121231.explanation: Years after the Roemer committee report, the housing situation for labor migrants is often still poor. Employers have a role here. Nevertheless, GL-PvdA does not see a lasting future for companies that depend on this.

121233.explanation: Wood burning is a significant public health problem, including in Oost Gelre. Therefore, burning is only allowed via the 'Stookwijzer' (burning guide), and new open fireplaces and wood stoves are discouraged.

121238.explanation: To enable the transition in agriculture, it is important that after the demolition of barns, the construction of a few homes remains possible (red-for-red/droebel policy) on or near the agricultural site. This benefits the livability of the countryside.

121240.explanation: The national government provides municipalities with a subsidy to help sustain the local broadcasting service. It is desirable that the municipality passes this subsidy on in full. However, according to GL-PvdA, there is no need to increase it from own funds.

121245.explanation: The number of BOAs (public enforcement officers) has been expanded and aligned with the tasks to be enforced over recent years. It is undesirable that they take over police duties. Therefore, it remains necessary to keep attention on maintaining sufficient police capacity.

121246.explanation: Camera surveillance in public spaces can be an important tool for maintaining public order, for example in nightlife areas. At present, there is no immediate cause, but expansion may be desirable in the future.

121252.explanation: The Participation Act aims to actively encourage people receiving benefits to find paid employment or, if that is not feasible, to participate actively in society in other ways, such as through volunteer work. Imposing obligations does not help.

121254.explanation: In practice, people with just above 120% of the social minimum fall outside minimum benefit schemes. Although every threshold is arbitrary, GL-PvdA considers it desirable to raise this limit to 130%.

121257.explanation: This is already a policy goal that Oost Gelre has set for itself. It's time to implement this through traffic plans. Particularly, main roads such as the Borculoseweg currently still have a 50 km/h limit, which is unsafe for cyclists and pedestrians.

121258.explanation: Regarding care provided under the Social Support Act (Wmo) and Youth Act, residents are bound to the contracted providers. Whether a provider can accommodate a preference for particular care workers is reasonably up to the provider to determine.

121260.explanation: In the coming years, the income the municipality receives from the national government will decrease. The property tax (OZB) therefore remains an important source of revenue that must certainly be maintained at its current level to sustain all municipal services.

121261.explanation: The municipality incurs costs for the few designated dog walking areas and emptying bins for dog waste. It is reasonable that dog owners contribute to these costs.

121262.explanation: Tourist tax does not need to be increased, aside from indexing, unless the financial situation in a few years warrants it.

121263.explanation: It is important that existing business parks are used more efficiently and revitalized. Additionally, there is no need for new space-consuming businesses, especially now that it is uncertain how sufficient employees can continue to be attracted in the future.

121266.explanation: Solar parks will remain an important contribution to large-scale sustainable energy production in our municipality in the future. Only 1 to 2 percent of agricultural land is involved. For farmers, solar parks can remain a supplement to their business operations if integrated into the greenery.

121269.explanation: Primary schools are free to choose, within existing teaching hours and financial resources, to pay attention to the Achterhoek dialect. The municipality can encourage this by promoting existing teaching packages to school boards.

121270.explanation: Drug use is undesirable and harmful to public health. The long-standing policy to prohibit the establishment of a coffee shop must therefore be maintained.

121271.explanation: In Oost Gelre, the share of existing social rental housing is significantly less than 28%. Nevertheless, there is indeed a need for it here. The previous municipal executive consciously built fewer social rental homes in recent years. GL-PvdA believes we should meet this standard in new building plans.

121272.explanation: To alleviate the housing shortage, creative options such as splitting homes and renting by the room are good possibilities for certain target groups. Within legal (safety) constraints, we must cooperate with this.

121273.explanation: Due to the increase in housing units such as apartments in the centers of Groenlo and Lichtenvoorde, parking pressure is rising sharply. Parking facilities within walking distance (possibly underground parking garages) should offer a future-oriented solution.

121274.explanation: The car-free market area and surroundings have brought much good (pleasant) public space to Groenlo. Visitors to the fortified town and shoppers would benefit if several other (shopping) streets, like Beltrumsestraat, were also made car-free.

121275.explanation: If artificial turf needs replacement after years of intensive use, replacement is unavoidable. Experience has shown that sports clubs cannot cover these investment costs (fully) from their own funds.

121276.explanation: Oost Gelre is an event municipality. Besides the Black Cross, there are the flower parade, carnival, the Slag om Grol, and numerous smaller events. Another large-scale event is not desired, also to avoid increasing the existing nuisance experienced by some residents.

121277.explanation: The municipality is responsible for local health policy. Nationally, it is recognized that alcohol, besides addiction, increases the risk of cancer.

121278.explanation: In the future, the small villages, as well as the two main centers Lichtenvoorde and Groenlo, will continue to play a role in realizing new housing developments. This way, the needs of house seekers can be met.

121279.explanation: At this moment, it is still unclear what the function of such a reporting center could be and how it is connected to the care and safety network. If it turns out that it can provide a clear added value, GL-PvdA has no objection to this.

121280.explanation: Despite national headwinds, GL-PvdA is convinced that a wind farm between Groenlo and Ruurlo can make a necessary contribution to the energy transition. This will be located at least 600 meters from individual homes and 2 kilometers from residential areas.

121224.explanation: Quality is important primarily, but the pursuit of balance between men and women is encouraged. Not party interests first, but the interests of the residents foremost. Politics without games should become the new normal. Practical, down to earth, and straightforward!

121225.explanation: For major subjects such as the widening of the N18, a referendum or citizen's assembly would be a good instrument. It creates more support among residents if ordinary citizens have been able to think along and weigh options. In addition, we take residents seriously and want to be transparent.

121230.explanation: National policy enforces the principle of distribution and solidarity for a fair allocation across the country. The shelter must not come at the expense of housing. The municipality must set strict conditions: maximum size, clear target group mix, extra security/enforcement, and integration capability.

121231.explanation: Those who recruit and deploy labor migrants are also responsible for the basic conditions, no shifting of costs to the municipality, housing corporations or neighbors. This reduces pressure on the local housing market for starters and families seeking scarce rental homes. It also provides calmness and livability in neighborhoods; responsible employer!

121233.explanation: No total ban, but rules do apply (heating guide/no burning during calm, misty weather; complaints protocol). Education (only dry wood, chimney sweeping, correct lighting method). Clean burning with soot filters helps. Residents retain the freedom to choose how to heat their homes. That cannot be prohibited!

121238.explanation: We support 'red for red' policy. Demolishing sheds to build houses and home units. More homes in rural areas are good for schools, clubs, and neighborhood solidarity. We look out for one another more. By demolishing empty sheds, we prevent criminals from pressuring farmers.

121240.explanation: The local broadcaster is of great importance to the community. They provide visibility for associations, culture, sports, and events. Initially, they can stabilize their operations through advertising and other revenues. If that fails, the municipality can subsidize as an exception.

121245.explanation: No troublemakers around the house! Nuisance from drug trafficking and antisocial behavior in neighborhoods must be tackled more firmly. OOG wants increased police and official surveillance in the evenings. Everyone deserves a safe neighborhood and a safe home. (Youth) crime must be tackled with an iron fist.

121246.explanation: No troublemakers around the house! OOG wants more evening surveillance by police and enforcement officers. Everyone deserves a safe neighborhood and safe home. Municipal camera surveillance is only a last resort and surrounded by much national legislation. It cannot be deployed arbitrarily, which is good.

121252.explanation: We support unpaid, socially useful activities that the municipality can request from someone receiving social welfare benefits. It concerns unpaid work that is socially useful, should fit the individual, be limited in duration and extent, and must not be a function normally compensated by a salary.

121254.explanation: Low-income earners often just fall outside support schemes. Earlier assistance has proven to prevent larger problems. Therefore, there must be provisions for those earning up to 130% above the social assistance standard. Prevention is better than cure.

121257.explanation: OOG advocates a maximum speed of 30 km/h on all roads within built-up areas. With road adjustments that do not hinder ambulance, fire brigade, police, and motorists. We have frequently urged the council through motions for this.

121258.explanation: Care must be closer and mutual. This fits this statement. It is important that care moves back from paperwork to practice. Faith and/or culture can help with that. It also supports longer independent living. Dementia-friendly neighborhoods also help.

121260.explanation: Housing costs are currently under pressure. The property tax (OZB) is the main factor. Year after year, we see the municipality ending with a surplus due to overestimating the budget, resulting in residents actually paying too much in property tax and other charges. That money should be visibly returned to citizens through proper spending.

121261.explanation: As long as dog tax is treated as general revenue, OOG favors abolishing it. Tax collection must benefit dog owners, for example: dog walking areas/off-leash zones and free poop bags.

121262.explanation: In Oost Gelre, tourists pay €1.47 per overnight stay. The national average is €2.53 per overnight. Increasing this amount could cause tourists to avoid Oost Gelre.

121263.explanation: We support entrepreneurs, certainly. But we do not want to expand industrial parks arbitrarily without first filling empty spots in existing industrial areas. Only then should new industrial land be made available.

121266.explanation: Solar panels should primarily be placed on roofs, especially in industrial areas. There is plenty of space, and the structures allow it. Current policy and experience (e.g., Zwolle solar park) show there is no enthusiasm among residents and the market for solar parks on agricultural land in Oost Gelre.

121269.explanation: It is not wrong to have appropriate pride in your “mother tongue.” We notice with dialect pop music that Achterhoeks certainly appeals to people from the Achterhoek. So, old habits die hard, and an hour a week within education can contribute to maintaining Achterhoek culture.

121270.explanation: As long as there is no request for it, we are very cautious and opposed. A coffeeshop attracts a certain type of tourism that causes nuisance and street dealing in the area. For Oost Gelre, drugs are a problem. We should not seek this by placing a coffeeshop.

121271.explanation: OOG stands for affordable housing. It is a right. Housing is a right. More people nowadays cannot buy a house and become dependent on the volatile private rental market (rents above €1100). Social rental housing is a must.

121272.explanation: In Oost Gelre, it is agreed that renting out an attic or floor is possible. Due to the current housing shortage, this is unfortunately necessary. OOG believes everyone should have the right to a home, not just a room. Otherwise, a whole generation loses their future.

121273.explanation: Parking standards should not frustrate housing development. OOG advocates 'full throttle' and 'minimal rules, maximum construction' in housing development. Parking standards should not create extra hurdles that make plans unfeasible, especially for (smaller) homes or infill. However, parking pressure must be prevented.

121274.explanation: A car-reduced environment should be created around the centers of Groenlo and Lichtenvoorde, with sufficient parking nearby and facilities for people with limited mobility.

121275.explanation: Sport is the engine of our society. It connects our residents and gives energy. Large investments in sports clubs must always be supported. OOG wants to greenify and avoid artificial turf fields. If clubs want to keep artificial turf, they must contribute financially.

121276.explanation: There are already many large-scale events in Oost Gelre that we are proud of: Carnival parades, Battle of Grolle, Flower Parade Lichtenvoorde, Spectacle Weekend Mariënvelde. We are an event municipality and where there is little or no nuisance and the event contributes to Oost Gelre, it is certainly possible.

121277.explanation: Without wanting to wag a finger, Oost Gelre is among the municipalities with the highest alcohol consumption, 81% (CBS figures). The dangers are often underestimated. With prevention, education, and enforcement, we can certainly do something for the health of our residents.

121278.explanation: Having an affordable home is a right. OOG stands for this. Building according to need and ensuring flow. Serving all residents within and around Oost Gelre. This is necessary and has the HIGHEST priority.

121279.explanation: Drug use in Oost Gelre is problematic. Help and prevention are certainly necessary. Enforcement as well. * Faster detection and targeted approach * Provides insight into nuisance situations * Strengthens the feeling of safety and trust * Links with care and prevention.

121280.explanation: As long as national guidelines and health factors for neighbors are unclear, it is unwise to place (large) wind turbines. We have always said: solar instead of wind – solar panels on roofs instead of windmills.

121224.explanation: OLW finds it important that women also hold high positions in the municipal government. Unfortunately, women still often face prejudices while having the same or better competencies than men.

121225.explanation: Residents already have much influence by electing representatives once every four years. Furthermore, in our view, citizens have sufficient other democratic tools, such as citizen initiatives, the right to speak, and petitions. We find that sufficient.

121230.explanation: This is not up to us as a municipality but is determined by The Hague. So, if The Hague requires an asylum seekers center (AZC), we believe the municipality must cooperate properly. This is what the (distribution) law prescribes.

121233.explanation: This must be prohibited during Red code advised by the Heating Guide, i.e., during misty, windless weather. In new builds and renovations, OLW also favors banning the installation of stoves.

121238.explanation: On farmyards, we think this should be allowed under certain conditions.

121240.explanation: The local broadcaster is important for our democracy and information provision.

121245.explanation: And also BOAs who patrol in the evenings and on weekends.

121246.explanation: We see no reason for that. Oost Gelre is a safe municipality.

121252.explanation: Agreed, as long as they are capable of doing so.

121254.explanation: Because at this moment OLW finds the municipal poverty policy insufficient.

121257.explanation: Completely agree. Much safer especially for children and the elderly.

121258.explanation: We believe that people are equal. Religion or culture should not create barriers in healthcare.

121260.explanation: In general, people with real estate have sufficient means to pay the property tax. With the property tax, the municipality does meaningful things for the community.

121261.explanation: As long as the municipality still incurs costs to empty dog waste bins and retrieve dog waste bags from green areas, in our opinion the dog tax should cover these costs.

121262.explanation: Many facilities that tourists also use can be better maintained or improved with this. Such as more benches, trash bins, signage, landscape maintenance, etc.

121263.explanation: We have enough business parks. More would damage the landscape and nature. Besides, there are enough jobs, so who are you doing it for?

121266.explanation: But then the municipality must require companies to install solar panels on their roofs.

121269.explanation: It is good for children to come into contact with the Achterhoek language and culture. But it should not be forced.

121270.explanation: Only if national legislation is introduced that allows this.

121271.explanation: OLW is in favor of more social rental housing.

121272.explanation: That depends on the situation and location. Renting out rooms usually means more nuisance in a street (for example, more cars).

121273.explanation: More parking spaces means less green and less play area for children. Cycling and walking must be promoted much more. We do support underground parking in Groenlo and Lichtenvoorde.

121274.explanation: Making the Markt car-free has proven to be a great success. As far as OLW is concerned, the Beltrumsestraat should have been made car-free at the same time.

121276.explanation: We think one large-scale event, alongside all other smaller events, is sufficient.

121277.explanation: Because both in Oost Gelre and the Achterhoek, nationally speaking, too much alcohol is consumed. This can especially lead to brain damage among young people.

121278.explanation: In our opinion, this must be considered per locality because needs differ.

121279.explanation: Fine to do that, although we believe that soft drugs such as cannabis should not be included. So only for hard drugs.

121280.explanation: But not in meadow bird areas. Yes along the N18 and in industrial areas.

121224.explanation: Quality is the most important criterion.

121225.explanation: The elected council has a mandate for the entire governing period.

121230.explanation: The arrival of an asylum seekers center can put too much pressure on local facilities, such as housing, care, and safety.

121231.explanation: Living at the workplace can lead to dependency and undesirable power relations.

121233.explanation: Allowing open fireplaces and wood stoves gives residents freedom in their living comfort and heating; advice is to follow the burning guide.

121238.explanation: Allowing farmers to build houses on their land can lead to uncontrolled housing construction, landscape degradation, and conflicts with spatial planning, while municipalities should maintain control over where and how building takes place.

121240.explanation: Additional support is needed to prevent local news from declining in quality.

121245.explanation: Increasing the number of BOAs brings structural costs, while safety can also be effectively improved through better cooperation with police and neighboring municipalities and by focusing on prevention, without appointing additional enforcement officers.

121246.explanation: More camera surveillance does not fit our vision of freedom and privacy.

121252.explanation: Obliging welfare recipients to engage in community work can lead to unnecessary pressure, while many people already actively participate in their community or seek work; additionally, this can be administratively complex and costly for the municipality.

121254.explanation: The limit is already 10% above the legal norm.

121257.explanation: Not on roads with separate bicycle paths.

121258.explanation: This is undesirable because it hinders integration.

121260.explanation: Lowering the property tax reduces municipal revenue, resulting in less money available for essential services and investments in livability, infrastructure, and care.

121261.explanation: The dog tax provides the municipality with income with which public space amenities, such as maintenance of greenery and dog walking areas, can be financed; abolition means these costs fall on all residents.

121262.explanation: Unless it is used for the maintenance of tourist facilities.

121263.explanation: We must focus on improving and modernizing existing business parks.

121266.explanation: Apply the solar ladder, meaning placing more solar panels on roofs. This preserves agricultural production capacity and the living environment.

121270.explanation: Allowing a coffeeshop in the municipality can lead to better supervision and regulation of cannabis sales and reduces illegal trade and nuisance.

121271.explanation: On average for the entire municipality but not per building location.

121272.explanation: This helps to reduce the housing shortage.

121275.explanation: Subsidizing the replacement of artificial turf fields places a significant financial burden on the municipal budget, while sports clubs can often reserve funds themselves or arrange alternative financing; municipal money is better spent on core tasks such as education, care, and infrastructure.

121276.explanation: Organizing another large-scale event alongside the Zwarte Cross can put pressure on residents' quality of life due to extra traffic congestion, noise nuisance, and environmental impact.

121277.explanation: There are national campaigns for this.

121278.explanation: Prioritizing housing construction in small villages can lead to inefficient use of facilities and infrastructure.

121279.explanation: Opening a reporting point for drug use can give residents a false sense of security and leads to additional costs and organizational burdens for the municipality.

121280.explanation: Installing wind turbines in Oost Gelre can harm the landscape and residents' living environment due to noise disturbance, shadow flicker, and the habitat of meadow birds.

121224.explanation: It must be of good quality.

121225.explanation: They are allowed to do that once every 4 years through the municipal elections.

121230.explanation: We adhere to national agreements, such as the targets in the Dispersion Act. As long as these apply, we must take our responsibility. We do this in a way that fits our municipality, where public support is of great importance. The safety of residents comes first.

121231.explanation: If entrepreneurs want to facilitate this, that is fine.

121238.explanation: What is possible: splitting houses, residential bubbles, and 'red for red' under conditions.

121240.explanation: We apply the national government grant.

121245.explanation: The size must be appropriate to its tasks.

121246.explanation: Where necessary, we will apply this within the framework of public order and safety.

121254.explanation: Where needed, we are willing to provide customized solutions.

121257.explanation: Where traffic flow is necessary, we maintain 50 km/h. Where safety is at risk, we apply 30 km/h.

121260.explanation: Property tax increase based on the national inflation rate. We keep the charges as low as possible.

121261.explanation: This must not lead to an increase in other taxes.

121262.explanation: Recreation and tourism are important to us. Increasing them should not be an end in itself. We want to remain attractive to tourists.

121263.explanation: Quickly develop new expansion locations for business parks with light industry and small plots.

121266.explanation: There is already a national ban.

121269.explanation: We should not interfere with the education curriculum.

121271.explanation: Build according to need. Affordable (social) homeownership must also be possible.

121272.explanation: The VVD agrees with the current policy.

121274.explanation: Making it further car-free discourages shopping visitors.

121275.explanation: This is the responsibility of the sports clubs together with the municipality.

121276.explanation: Allowed if there is demand. We are a municipality that warmly supports events and welcomes initiatives.

121277.explanation: Everyone has their own responsibility here.

121278.explanation: Build according to need. In all villages there is a need to add homes.

121279.explanation: This can be reported to the police.

121280.explanation: If we are legally required to cooperate, we will do so.

121224.explanation: Qualities are more important than male/female.

121230.explanation: In the small communities, we do not want asylum reception, and in Lichtenvoorde/Groenlo no permanent reception locations.

121231.explanation: It can be an option for all entrepreneurs, including those outside agriculture. But not mandatory. It must fit with the environment.

121233.explanation: Fully agree, this must not be taken away.

121238.explanation: Tailor-made solutions are already applied in our municipality.

121240.explanation: This strengthens the social bonds of the inhabitants of the region.

121246.explanation: If necessary and if it improves safety.

121252.explanation: We have to do it together. It is good that these residents are involved in society.

121254.explanation: Our municipality is already generous and applies standards above the legal minimum.

121257.explanation: That is already the case almost everywhere.

121258.explanation: We support an inclusive society, with respect and tolerance towards all people.

121260.explanation: Local government cannot function without money.

121262.explanation: The tourist tax may only be increased by a maximum of 2% per year following the trend.

121266.explanation: Solar panels on roofs, not on fertile agricultural land.

121269.explanation: It is not the core task of the municipality, but it is nice that it is cherished.

121270.explanation: We prefer not to have a coffeeshop, but we do not completely rule it out.

121271.explanation: Building for starters and the elderly will help stimulate housing flow. A relocation coach can play an important role in this.

121272.explanation: We expect too many negative side effects.

121273.explanation: Especially in Groenlo it is desirable.

121274.explanation: Partly it is already car-free. The shops must remain easily accessible.

121275.explanation: Provided it is necessary and the association also contributes to this themselves.

121276.explanation: That could be an option, but not a goal in itself.

121277.explanation: It is not the task of the municipality.

121278.explanation: Affordable housing must be built everywhere in our municipality as much as possible in the near future.

121279.explanation: It is unnecessary since reports can be made directly to the police.

121280.explanation: We support the arrival of small-scale wind turbines/farm turbines, provided they offer direct benefits to our residents/businesses. We are not in favor of large wind turbines/parks.

statements.title: The municipality should give money to sports clubs to replace existing artificial turf pitches.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Oost Gelre should open a reporting point for drug use.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: In Oost Gelre the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Oost Gelre moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The residents of Oost Gelre should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Oost Gelre moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: In Oost Gelre there should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.text: In Oost Gelre moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The center of Groenlo should be car-free.
Housing development in the small villages should from now on have priority over that in Lichtenvoorde and Groenlo.

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

titleParts.text: moet voortaan voorrang krijgen op die in Lichtenvoorde en Groenlo.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Achterhoeks dialect.
In Oost Gelre there should be more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
In addition to the Zwarte Cross there should be another large-scale event in Oost Gelre.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: More business parks should be built in Oost Gelre.
Of the new homes to be built in Oost Gelre, at least 30% should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Oost Gelre moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should actively discourage the consumption of alcohol.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must cooperate in placing wind turbines in Oost Gelre.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meewerken aan de plaatsing van

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
